gaps: The statpack has no section for mandatory appeals / jurisdictional statements; the modern discretionary-cert base rates do not describe the section 1253 population this event belongs to.
fedcourts query has no topic/subject filter that would surface redistricting or VRA priors; a generic --court scotus query returned unrelated cert dockets.
A decided-docket guard at provisioning time (like outcome.py's termination_signal) would have caught that this forward cell's snapshot already contains the disposition, before fanning out predictors.
helpful: metrics/statpack.md for the overall SCOTUS disposition context
fedcourts query --corpus-backend ranged worked without a local corpus pull
notes: Cell decided on the provisioned snapshot itself; retrieval added little because the docket record (hold-for-Callais pattern, expedite/stay motions) was decisive — and the snapshot already contained the outcome (flagged).
